key_risk_factors: Privacy risk – customer data may be used for product improvement unless a manual opt‑out is submitted and no region‑specific data residency is currently offered.
Compliance risk – only SOC 2 Type I certification (no Type II, no ISO 27001, GDPR/CCPA attestations), which may not satisfy regulatory requirements for financial documents.
Operational risk – multi‑tenant cloud deployment with a 99.5 % uptime SLA and limited documented disaster‑recovery or incident‑response procedures.
Vendor maturity risk – company has been operating for only 18 months with 12 enterprise customers, indicating limited track record and support depth.
Pricing risk – usage‑based model can scale dramatically with document volume and rate‑limit thresholds are undocumented, creating cost‑predictability issues.
missing_information: Exact timeline and details for upcoming region‑specific data residency options.
Automated opt‑out mechanism for data‑use in model training and associated data‑retention policy.
Roadmap to achieve SOC 2 Type II (or other) certification and any existing ISO 27001, ISO 27701, GDPR/CCPA compliance statements.
Detailed SLA terms, including penalties for downtime, and documented disaster‑recovery / business‑continuity plans.
Comprehensive pricing schedule, volume‑discount tiers, and clear API rate‑limit specifications.
Encryption key management model (customer‑managed vs. vendor‑managed) and key rotation practices.
Third‑party audit reports, security incident history, and references from existing enterprise customers.
business_recommendation: Proceed with a structured pilot only after obtaining the missing information and negotiating stronger contractual safeguards. Require a data‑residency addendum, an automated opt‑out process, and explicit cost‑cap provisions. Insist on SOC 2 Type II (or equivalent) certification before full production use, and consider alternative vendors with proven maturity and clearer pricing models as a fallback.
